skill.name: python-sandbox
skill.description: Run Python workflows in a sandbox for data analysis, visualization, machine learning, and document generation.
skill.summary: Generate and execute sandboxed Python workflows for analysis, charts, reports, and files.
skill.author: CK991357

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe patterns, but targeted review found no confirmed malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, or active network exfiltration. Most high-volume hits are Markdown or benign cookbook false positives; the remaining real concern is that the skill intentionally guides sandboxed Python execution and `/data` file generation.

medium_findings.title: Sandboxed Python Code Execution Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to guide Python code execution for data analysis, visualization, machine learning, and document generation. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but generated code execution is an elevated-risk capability that requires sandboxing and review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The core instructions explicitly describe a Python execution environment and also document blocked dangerous operations. The risk is real but bounded by the stated sandbox controls.

medium_findings.title: Workspace File Read and Write Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read and write files under `/data` and to generate downloadable artifacts. This supports the skill purpose but can expose sensitive data if users provide confidential inputs or if generated files are mishandled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are explicit and central to report generation. The documented scope is the sandbox workspace, so this is a controlled but meaningful risk.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Most external command findings come from Markdown backticks and examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. The notable system-command example is inside a prohibited operations block that says it will be blocked.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown formatting, data-structure examples, or a negative example. I found no evidence that the skill instructs shell command execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Are Semantic False Positives
low_findings.description: Representative high and low blocker hits refer to ordinary words or APIs in analytics examples, such as graph node source labels, residual calculations, random forest parameters, SciPy integration, and SymPy function definitions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review of the cited areas shows benign scientific and visualization code. The static labels appear to match substrings rather than security-relevant behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Appear Only in Sample HTML
low_findings.description: The network findings cite example hyperlinks inside a static HTML parsing demonstration. I found no evidence that the skill performs network requests or exfiltrates data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited URLs are literal anchor tags in sample HTML content. They are not used by request code or any active network client.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sandboxed Python Code Execution Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill is designed to guide Python code execution for data analysis, visualization, machine learning, and document generation. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but generated code execution is an elevated-risk capability that requires sandboxing and review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The core instructions explicitly describe a Python execution environment and also document blocked dangerous operations. The risk is real but bounded by the stated sandbox controls.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workspace File Read and Write Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read and write files under `/data` and to generate downloadable artifacts. This supports the skill purpose but can expose sensitive data if users provide confidential inputs or if generated files are mishandled.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are explicit and central to report generation. The documented scope is the sandbox workspace, so this is a controlled but meaningful risk.

content.user_title: Analyze Data in a Python Sandbox
content.value_statement: Complex analysis tasks often need code, charts, and generated files. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through sandboxed Python workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides pandas, NumPy, SciPy, SymPy, and scikit-learn workflows.
Creates charts with Matplotlib, Seaborn, Graphviz, and NetworkX.
Generates Excel, Word, PDF, and PowerPoint files from Python output.
Explains sandbox file handling with `/data` workspace paths and Base64 context files.
Provides cookbook patterns for text extraction, reporting, and machine learning.
limitations: Requires a working Python sandbox tool with the documented libraries installed.
Network access is described as disabled, so remote data fetching is not supported.
Large files and complex models must stay within memory and timeout limits.
Generated code should be reviewed before use with sensitive or regulated data.

use_cases.title: Clean and summarize business data
use_cases.description: Load spreadsheet or CSV data, clean fields, calculate metrics, and produce a chart or workbook.

use_cases.title: Prototype a model workflow
use_cases.description: Prepare sample data, train a baseline model, evaluate results, and visualize feature impact.

use_cases.title: Automate report generation
use_cases.description: Turn processed data and visualizations into PDF, Word, Excel, or presentation deliverables.

prompt_templates.title: Start a basic analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Python sandbox to inspect `/data/data.csv`, summarize the columns, identify missing values, and create one useful chart.

prompt_templates.title: Create a clean workbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Clean `/data/sales.xlsx`, calculate monthly totals by region, and generate a downloadable Excel workbook with charts and summary sheets.

prompt_templates.title: Build a model report
prompt_templates.prompt: Train a baseline model from `/data/training.csv`, evaluate performance, explain the most important features, and create a concise PDF report.

prompt_templates.title: Design a multi-step pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a staged Python workflow that caches intermediate files in `/data`, validates inputs, produces visualizations, and exports final Word and PowerPoint reports.

output_examples.input: Analyze a sales spreadsheet and create a management summary.
output_examples.output: A short statistical summary, a regional revenue chart, and a downloadable Excel report with cleaned data and totals.

output_examples.input: Compare model performance on a tabular classification dataset.
output_examples.output: Accuracy metrics, confusion matrix guidance, feature importance notes, and a PDF report for stakeholders.

output_examples.input: Extract structured facts from an HTML document.
output_examples.output: A cleaned table of extracted fields, validation notes, and a text summary of important findings.

best_practices: Use only trusted input files and inspect generated code before execution.
Store intermediate outputs in `/data` and keep file names clear and stable.
Process large files in chunks and remove unused variables to control memory use.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to execute untrusted arbitrary code.
Do not place secrets, credentials, or private keys in analysis files.
Do not assume network access or system-level file access is available.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides sandboxed Python workflows for analysis, visualization, machine learning, and document generation.

faq.question: Does it need a Python execution tool?
faq.answer: Yes. It is useful only when the assistant can call a compatible Python sandbox.

faq.question: Can it create downloadable files?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions cover Excel, Word, PDF, PowerPoint, and chart outputs.

faq.question: Can it access the internet?
faq.answer: The skill documentation says network access is disabled in the sandbox.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Generated code and files should be reviewed before processing sensitive data.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
